originalTitle: Trump news at a glance: president claims Iran ‘no longer want a nuclear weapon’ amid peace deal hopes
summary: Washington and Tehran express increasing optimism that weeks of halting negotiations may be drawing to a close – key US politics stories from Saturday 13 June at a glance
content: Donald Trump says a deal with Iran to end the war would be signed on Sunday, and that the strait of Hormuz would be “open to all” immediately after. Iran had offered a different timeline earlier in the day, but nonetheless signalled an agreement was in the offing, as both the warring parties and their mediators expressed increasing optimism that weeks of halting negotiations were drawing to a close. “The Deal is scheduled to get signed tomorrow, and immediately after it is signed, the Hormuz Strait is OPEN TO ALL,” Trump said on his Truth Social platform. Since an 8 April truce paused the worst of the fighting, Trump has repeatedly insisted a deal was near only for the wrangling to drag on. The Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baqaei, had said earlier on Saturday that the date of the signing was yet to be determined, but “it will not be tomorrow”. However, he added: “The possibility of this happening in the coming days cannot be ruled out.” Trump says Iran peace deal could be signed by Sunday, with strait of Hormuz to open shortly after Trump has claimed that the deal would prevent Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon, as well as reopen the strait of Hormuz to international shipping. In a Truth Social post, Trump said that Iran “no longer want a Nuclear Weapon, nor will they have one, either through purchase, development, or any other form of procurement”. Read the full story Workers remove Trump’s name from Kennedy Center after court rulings Trump’s name has been removed from the facade of the Kennedy Center in Washington DC, hours after a judge rejected an emergency appeal to block the removal of the president’s name. Read the full story Marjorie Taylor Greene criticizes Trump’s UFC event at White House Marjorie Taylor Greene has criticized Trump’s plan to hold a UFC fight on the White House lawn, as the president prepares to host seven fights on Sunday. The former rightwing Republican congresswoman, a once fierce defender of Trump who turned on him towards the end of her time in office, told NewsNation the location is inappropriate for the mixed martial arts event. chineseTitle: 特朗普新闻一览：总统声称伊朗“不再想要核武器”，希望达成和平协议
chineseSummary: 华盛顿和德黑兰对持续数周的谈判可能即将结束表示越来越乐观——6 月 13 日星期六的美国重要政治新闻一览
chineseContent: 唐纳德·特朗普表示，将于周日与伊朗签署结束战争的协议，霍尔木兹海峡将立即“向所有人开放”。伊朗当天早些时候提出了不同的时间表，但仍表示即将达成协议，因为交战双方及其调解人都对中断数周的谈判即将结束表示越来越乐观。特朗普在他的真相社交平台上表示：“该协议定于明天签署，签署后，霍尔木兹海峡立即向所有人开放。”自从 4 月 8 日的休战协议暂停了最严重的战斗以来，特朗普一再坚称即将达成协议，但争吵却一直在拖延。伊朗外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴卡伊(Esmaeil Baqaei)周六早些时候表示，签署日期尚未确定，但“不会是明天”。不过，他补充道：“不能排除未来几天发生这种情况的可能性。”特朗普表示，伊朗和平协议可能会在周日签署，霍尔木兹海峡将开放，此前特朗普声称该协议将阻止德黑兰获得核武器，并重新向国际航运开放霍尔木兹海峡。特朗普在《真相社会》帖子中表示，伊朗“不再想要核武器，也不会通过购买、开发或任何其他形式的采购”。阅读全文 法院裁定特朗普的名字已从华盛顿特区肯尼迪中心的正面删除，工作人员将特朗普的名字从肯尼迪中心删除，几小时前，一名法官驳回了阻止删除总统名字的紧急上诉。阅读全文 马乔里·泰勒·格林 (Marjorie Taylor Greene) 批评特朗普在白宫举办的 UFC 赛事 马乔里·泰勒·格林 (Marjorie Taylor Greene) 批评了特朗普的计划
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
